title: Apply Now: Oxford University Postdoctoral Fellowship in African Studies 2026
summary: The University of Oxford is inviting applications for a Postdoctoral Research Fellow in African Studies at the Oxford School of Global and Area Studies (OSGA). This is a prestigious opportunity for early-career scholars to advance their research within a globally recognized academic environment. Overview of the Fellowship Position: Postdoctoral Research Fellow in African Studies Institution: African Studies Centre (ASC), OSGA Location: Oxford , United Kingdom Salary: £39,424 – £47,779 per annum (Grade 7) Contract Type: Full-time (37.5 hours/week), 2-year fixed-term Start Date: 1 October 2026 (or as soon as possible thereafter) Application Deadline: 20 April 2026 (12:00 noon UK time) About the Fellowship The African Studies Centre at Oxford is seeking an outstanding early-career researcher to develop an innovative and independent research programme under the broad theme of “Africa and the World.” The fellowship focuses on understanding Africa’s place within contemporary global, comparative, and transnational contexts. Working under the supervision of Rita Abrahamsen , the successful candidate will contribute to advancing cutting-edge scholarship and strengthening Oxford’s position as a leading hub for African Studies. Key Responsibilities The selected Fellow will: Conduct an independent and ambitious research programme in African Studies Publish high-quality research in peer-reviewed journals and academic presses Present research at conferences, seminars, and academic forums Contribute to shaping the “Africa and the World” research agenda at Oxford Organize seminars, conferences, and collaborative research initiatives Teach an option course aligned with their expertise Supervise master’s dissertations in the MSc in African Studies Actively participate in the academic community at OSGA and ASC Benefits of the Position Working at the University of Oxford offers a comprehensive benefits package, including: 38 days of annual leave (inclusive of public holidays), with options to extend Generous family leave schemes , including maternity, adoption, and paternity leave A competitive pension scheme Sustainable commuting options , including cycle loans and discounted travel Access to a vibrant community of social, cultural, and sports clubs Eligibility Criteria Applicants should: Hold (or be close to completing) a relevant doctoral degree Have completed their doctorate no earlier than three years before 1 January 2026 Demonstrate strong academic writing and publication potential Show experience presenting research at conferences and seminars Have a commitment to teaching and postgraduate supervision Desirable Experience Teaching and supervising theses Leading independent research projects Collaborating with other researchers Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion The Oxford School of Global and Area Studies is committed to fostering an inclusive academic environment. Applications are particularly encouraged from women and individuals from Black and minority ethnic backgrounds, who are underrepresented in academic roles at Oxford. The School also holds a Bronze Athena Swan Award , recognizing its commitment to advancing gender equality. Application Process Candidates must apply through the official Oxford University recruitment portal. The application requires: A Curriculum Vitae (CV) A supporting statement addressing the selection criteria A research proposal outlining the proposed project Applicants are advised not to submit published work at this stage. Read more and Apply Here Important Dates Application Deadline: 20 April 2026 (12:00 noon UK time) Interview Date: 14 May 2026 (online) This fellowship presents a unique opportunity to join a globally respected institution and contribute to influential research on Africa’s role in the world. Scholars with strong, original research ideas and a commitment to academic excellence are highly encouraged to apply. Discover more similar opportunities Here . title: NSF TechAccess: AI-Ready America Coordination Hubs — Up to $1M/Year to Expand AI Skills and Access
summary: The U.S. National Science Foundation (NSF), in partnership with the United States Department of Agriculture (USDA NIFA), the United States Department of Labor , and the Small Business Administration , is inviting applications for the TechAccess: AI-Ready America Coordination Hubs initiative. This major funding opportunity supports the creation of state- and territory-based hubs designed to expand AI access, literacy, and workforce readiness across the United States. About the Initiative The TechAccess program aims to strengthen the U.S. workforce and economy by ensuring that individuals, businesses, and communities are equipped to understand and adopt artificial intelligence technologies. Selected Coordination Hubs will serve as regional centers for AI education, training, and adoption support , helping bridge the gap between emerging technologies and real-world application. What the Hubs Will Do Funded hubs will: Provide AI literacy and foundational education programs Deliver applied AI skills training for workers and businesses Offer technical assistance to support AI adoption Facilitate hands-on learning opportunities, including internships and project-based experiences Strengthen regional innovation ecosystems and workforce development Funding Details Award amount: Up to $1,000,000 per year per hub Duration: 3 years (with potential for a 4th year) Funding supports large-scale, collaborative, and impact-driven initiatives Who Should Apply This opportunity is targeted at organizations capable of leading regional initiatives, including: Universities and research institutions Nonprofits and workforce development organizations Public-private partnerships State or territory-based entities Applicants should demonstrate the capacity to coordinate multi-stakeholder efforts and deliver measurable impact in AI readiness. Why This Opportunity Matters As AI continues to reshape industries, access to skills and tools becomes critical. This initiative ensures that workers, small businesses, and underserved communities are not left behind in the digital transformation. It is a strategic investment in building an inclusive, AI-ready workforce and economy. Application Deadline 📅 June 23, 2026 (5:00 PM EDT) Informational Webinar 📅 Date: April 14, 2026 ⏰ Time: 1:00 PM EDT 🔗 Register here. For more Grant Opportunities, Visit OFY here. title: AI Fellows Program 2026 at Gates Foundation India: Apply to Work on High-Impact AI Solutions
summary: The Bill Melinda Gates Foundation India office is now accepting applications for its first-ever AI Fellows cohort —a unique opportunity to apply artificial intelligence to real-world challenges in health, agriculture, and development. This 12-month fellowship places selected candidates on high-impact projects, where they will work closely with program teams and receive structured mentorship while building innovative AI-driven solutions. About the Program Each fellow will be embedded within Strategic Initiatives or Programmatic Strategy Teams, contributing to priority projects that address critical development challenges. Fellows will also be supported by experienced mentors and gain exposure to interdisciplinary collaboration across sectors. Fellowship Focus Areas Five fellows will be selected, each working on a specialized AI application area: AI Fellow #1: Maternal Fetal Health Risk Stratification Work with longitudinal pregnancy datasets to improve maternal and fetal health outcomes. Develop AI models for early detection of conditions such as gestational diabetes and preeclampsia. Build prototype risk-scoring tools for frontline healthcare decision-making. AI Fellow #2: AI Agricultural Advisory Integrate weather, satellite, and agronomic data into advisory tools for farmers. Develop conversational AI interfaces (voice and text) for smallholder engagement. Support climate risk prediction and crop advisory systems. AI Fellow #3: AI for Immunization Coverage Apply AI/ML to identify underserved populations (zero-dose and dropout children). Develop targeting algorithms and dashboards to improve vaccination outreach. Enhance last-mile delivery and monitoring systems. AI Fellow #4: AI for Drug Discovery Explore AI applications in target identification, molecule design, and screening. Work with multimodal datasets including genomics and clinical data. Collaborate with research institutions to strengthen AI-driven discovery pipelines. AI Fellow #5: Rapid Prototyping of Applied AI Solutions Develop AI prototypes across sectors like health, education, and agriculture. Use tools such as chatbots, knowledge graphs, and AI frameworks. Promote responsible AI practices and build internal toolkits and learning resources. Benefits Hands-on experience working on real-world AI projects Mentorship from experts within the foundation Exposure to global development challenges and interdisciplinary teams Opportunity to build scalable, impactful AI solutions Access to a strong professional network Why This Opportunity Matters This fellowship goes beyond theory—offering a chance to directly contribute to solutions that improve health systems, food security, and economic opportunities . It is ideal for individuals who want to use AI for meaningful, large-scale impact. How to Apply 🔗 Apply here For more Fellowship opportunities, visit OFY here. title: Call for Proposals: ACE Microgrants 2026 for African Climate and Environmental Initiatives
summary: The Pulitzer Center is now inviting applications for the Africa Climate and Environment (ACE) Microgrants 2026 , an exciting opportunity designed to support African local movements working at the intersection of climate, environment, and governance. About the ACE Microgrants The ACE Microgrants are part of the Pulitzer Center’s Africa outreach program, aimed at funding innovative engagement projects that creatively incorporate stories supported by the Center. These projects are expected to drive meaningful conversations and action around environmental transparency, accountability, and governance . By leveraging storytelling and community engagement, the initiative seeks to deepen public understanding of critical environmental and climate challenges across Africa. What Kind of Projects Are Supported? Applicants are encouraged to submit creative and impactful outreach projects that engage: Young professionals Local communities Stakeholders across sectors Projects should focus on key issues such as: Government management of natural resources Harmful industrial and corporate practices Conflicts of interest and corruption Impacts on biodiversity and ecosystems Indigenous communities and human rights Climate change and environmental sustainability Successful proposals will demonstrate the ability to: Amplify community voices and lived experiences Encourage dialogue among media, government, civil society, and businesses Inspire action through storytelling and public engagement Why You Should Apply This is a unique opportunity to: Secure funding for your climate or environmental initiative Engage communities in meaningful dialogue and advocacy Collaborate across sectors to influence policy and governance Contribute to transparency and accountability in Africa’s environmental landscape If you re part of a local movement passionate about climate action and governance, the ACE Microgrants could be your chance to turn ideas into impactful change. Eligible Applicants The program is open to a wide range of African local movements , including: Civil society organizations Media organizations Social enterprises Think tanks Community-based associations Types of Activities The ACE Microgrants support diverse outreach formats, such as: Conferences and workshops Exhibitions and storytelling events Awareness campaigns Community dialogues and forums Projects should be inspired by Pulitzer Center-supported reporting on environmental governance and transparency . Language Requirements Applications can be submitted in: English French Application Deadline April 30, 2026 APPLY HERE Interested applicants are strongly encouraged to review the official guidelines for detailed eligibility criteria and the selection process before applying. Discover more similar opportunities here. The post Call for Proposals: ACE Microgrants 2026 for African Climate and Environmental Initiatives appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Global Conservation Lead Role at the Crop Trust: Drive Global Action on Food Plant Biodiversity
summary: The Crop Trust is hiring a Global Conservation Lead to play a key role in advancing global efforts to protect and conserve food plant biodiversity. This is an exciting opportunity for professionals passionate about sustainability, agriculture, and global conservation to contribute to a high-impact international initiative. About the Role The Global Conservation Lead will coordinate engagement within the Global Conservation Consortium for Food Plants (GCCFP) —a major global initiative focused on safeguarding plant diversity critical for food security. This role involves working with international partners, coordinating conservation strategies, and helping drive forward collaborative efforts to protect vital plant species. Key Responsibilities Lead coordination of activities within the GCCFP Engage with global stakeholders and partners Support strategic planning and implementation of conservation initiatives Drive collaboration across institutions and regions Contribute to advancing global food security and biodiversity goals Who Should Apply This role is ideal for professionals with: Experience in conservation, agriculture, or environmental science Strong project management and coordination skills Interest in global sustainability and biodiversity Ability to work with international teams and stakeholders Why This Opportunity Matters Food plant diversity is essential for global food systems, climate resilience, and sustainable development. This role offers a chance to contribute directly to preserving biodiversity and ensuring food security for future generations. How to Apply 🔗 Apply here. For more Job roles, Visit OFY here. title: Tax Fairness Fellowship 2026: Paid Opportunity for Professionals Shaping Fairer Tax Systems
summary: The Tax Fairness Fellowship is now open for applications, offering a paid, career-transition opportunity for professionals ready to shape fairer and more equitable tax systems. This unique fellowship is designed for experienced individuals who want to pivot into impactful work around tax justice, economic fairness, and public policy reform . About the Fellowship The Tax Fairness Fellowship brings together a diverse cohort of professionals from different sectors to build expertise and contribute to fairer tax systems globally. It is especially suited for individuals looking to transition into the tax justice space , regardless of whether their previous experience directly relates to taxation. The programme values transferable skills, creativity, and real-world impact over traditional career paths. Who Should Apply This fellowship is ideal for professionals with 5+ years of experience in areas such as: Tax law Finance or economics Public policy Advocacy and campaigns Communications and storytelling Community organizing Movement or coalition building Fundraising Applicants with non-linear career paths or career changers are strongly encouraged to apply. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Have at least 5 years of professional experience Demonstrate strong problem-solving and leadership abilities Be mission-driven and committed to social impact Be authorized to work in one of the following regions: Canada European Union Kenya United States Benefits Paid fellowship opportunity Career transition support into the tax justice field Exposure to global economic and policy issues Opportunity to work on meaningful, real-world challenges Access to a network of experts, advocates, and changemakers Why This Opportunity Matters Tax systems play a critical role in shaping inequality and economic opportunity. This fellowship empowers professionals to influence policy, advocate for fairness, and contribute to systemic change . If you re looking to shift your career toward meaningful impact, this is a powerful entry point into the world of tax justice. Application Deadline 📅 April 27, 2026 How to Apply 🔗 Apply here For more Fellowship opportunities, Visit OFY here. title: Attend Oxford Africa Conference 2026: Key Speakers, Agenda and How to Get Tickets
summary: The Oxford Africa Conference 2026 returns for its 16th edition, bringing together some of the most influential voices shaping Africa’s future. Hosted at the prestigious University of Oxford by the Oxford University Africa Society , this year’s conference promises two days of powerful dialogue, networking, and action-driven insights. Event Details Venue: Mathematical Institute, University of Oxford (Andrew Wiles Building) Dates: Saturday, 16 May 2026 – Sunday, 17 May 2026 Theme: Anchoring Africa: Grounded Leadership in the Age of Disruption Tickets: Available now — secure your spot early as tickets sell out fast Welcome to Oxford Africa Conference 2026 The Oxford Africa Conference continues to serve as a leading platform for thought leadership on Africa’s most pressing issues. This year’s theme reflects a critical moment in Africa’s journey—marked by rapid shifts in politics, economics, technology, and climate. Over two impactful days in Oxford, the conference will convene leaders across policy, business, finance, academia, and culture to: Share diverse perspectives Challenge prevailing assumptions Foster meaningful collaborations The goal is simple yet powerful: ensure conversations translate into long-term impact across Africa and the global African community. Conference Impact Beyond dialogue, the conference is committed to turning ideas into action . By spotlighting bold innovations and supporting transformative initiatives, the Oxford Africa Conference continues to catalyze change across the continent. Keynote Speaker One of the headline speakers for 2026 is: Julius Maada Bio – President of the Republic of Sierra Leone Expect insights from top-level leadership on governance, development, and Africa’s evolving global role. Featured Panels The conference features a diverse lineup of panels addressing Africa’s most critical sectors: Grounded Governance – Public trust, institutional reform, and state capacity Engines of Growth – Economic transformation and investment opportunities Founders in Focus – Entrepreneurship and innovation ecosystems Narrating Africa – Storytelling, media, and cultural influence Powering Africa s Energy Futures – Sustainable energy solutions Building Africa s Digital Capital – Technology and digital transformation Spotlight Panel: Grounded Governance This session explores how African states can rebuild trust, strengthen accountability, and deliver services effectively at scale. Fireside Chats Networking Engage in intimate, unscripted conversations with top leaders during fireside chats designed to go beyond surface-level discussions. Social Experiences Gala Dinner: A unique opportunity to dine and connect with speakers and delegates in a historic Oxford college setting AfroBop After Party: Celebrate African culture, music, and community in a vibrant closing event Featured Speakers Meet a dynamic lineup of innovators and changemakers, including: Theo Baloyi – Founder Group CEO, Bathu Peace Hyde – Creator Executive Producer of Young, Famous African Ron Chari – Policy and governance leader Rose M. Mutiso – Founder, African Tech Futures Lab More speakers and moderators will be announced soon. Accommodation Options Attendees can choose from a range of convenient and comfortable accommodations near the venue, including: Rhodes Trust Vanbrugh House Hotel Courtyard by Marriott Oxford Voco Oxford Spires Additional options include The Head of the River and Bear and Ragged Staff. Travel Information The conference has partnered with Kenya Airways as the official travel partner. Discount: 15% off flights Promo Code: OAC26KE Booking Window: Now until 17 May 2026 Travel Dates: 9 May – 24 May 2026 Delegates are encouraged to book early to take advantage of this exclusive offer. Secure Your Spot Be part of a transformative experience shaping Africa’s future. With limited seats available and high demand expected, early registration is strongly recommended. Get your tickets now and join the conversation driving Africa forward. Discover more similar opportunities Here. title: TWAS Fellowships & Research Opportunities 2026: Fully Funded PhD, Postdoctoral Visits to Germany, and Global Research Collaboration Programs
summary: The World Academy of Sciences (TWAS), in partnership with leading global institutions such as the German Research Foundation (DFG) and the Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR) India, is offering a range of fully funded fellowship and research opportunities for 2026. These programmes are designed to support scientists from developing countries through advanced research training, international collaboration, and capacity building. This comprehensive guide covers three major TWAS opportunities: TWAS-DFG Cooperation Visits Programme (Sub-Saharan Africa) TWAS-DFG Cooperation Visits Programme (MENA Region) TWAS-CSIR Postgraduate Fellowship Programme (PhD in India) Each programme provides unique benefits, from short-term research visits in Germany to fully funded doctoral studies in India. 1. TWAS-DFG Cooperation Visits Programme (Sub-Saharan Africa) The TWAS-DFG Cooperation Visits Programme for Sub-Saharan Africa offers postdoctoral researchers the opportunity to undertake a fully funded three-month research visit to Germany. Programme Overview Duration: 3 months Host Country: Germany Target Group: Postdoctoral researchers in Sub-Saharan Africa Deadline: April 15, 2026 The programme is designed to initiate and strengthen research collaborations between African scientists and German institutions, with the goal of building long-term academic partnerships. Key Benefits Participants will receive: Full travel expenses to Germany Subsistence allowance during the stay Access to advanced research facilities Opportunity to establish international research collaborations Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Hold a PhD obtained in 2021 or later Special extension for women: up to 6 additional years depending on number of children Be currently employed at a research institution in Sub-Saharan Africa Have at least 5 years of experience in the science system Not already be in Germany at the time of application Not have an established collaboration with the German host Required Documents Applicants must submit: Passport copy Invitation letter from a German host institution Two reference letters PhD certificate Proof of language proficiency (English or German) Institutional support letter Important Notes Visits must take place within 12 months of award No family support is provided Only one TWAS application is allowed per year Learn more and apply here 2. TWAS-DFG Cooperation Visits Programme (MENA Region) This programme mirrors the Sub-Saharan Africa version but is tailored for researchers in the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region. Programme Overview Duration: 3 months Host Country: Germany Target Group: Postdoctoral researchers in MENA Deadline: April 15, 2026 The goal is to foster collaboration between MENA researchers and German scientists. Key Benefits Travel funding Living expenses covered Access to German research institutions Opportunity for long-term collaboration Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Hold a PhD (2021 or later, with extensions for women) Be employed in a MENA-based institution Have at least 5 years of research or academic experience Not have prior collaboration with the host institution Application Requirements Similar to the SSA programme, including: Invitation letter References Academic credentials Language proof Institutional support Additional Notes Women scientists are strongly encouraged to apply All academic disciplines are eligible Collaboration must be newly initiated Learn more and apply here 3. TWAS-CSIR Postgraduate Fellowship Programme (PhD in India) The TWAS-CSIR Postgraduate Fellowship Programme offers fully funded PhD opportunities for students from developing countries to study in India. Programme Overview Level: PhD Duration: Up to 4 years Host Country: India Deadline: May 6, 2026 This programme supports research in cutting-edge areas of science and technology. Fields of Study Eligible disciplines include: Agricultural Sciences Biology and Biotechnology Medical and Health Sciences Chemistry Engineering Physics Mathematics Astronomy and Earth Sciences Fellowship Types Full-Time Fellowship For applicants not enrolled in a PhD Duration: Up to 4 years Sandwich Fellowship For PhD students already enrolled in their home country Duration: 6 months to 3 years Benefits Monthly stipend Subsidized accommodation Health insurance Access to CSIR research facilities Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Be under 35 years old Be nationals of developing countries (excluding India) Hold a Master’s degree in a relevant field Be employed in their home country Provide proof of English proficiency Secure acceptance from a CSIR institution Required Documents Acceptance letter from CSIR institution Academic certificates Research proposal Proof of employment English proficiency Important Notes Applicants must return to their home country after completion No concurrent assignments allowed Only one TWAS programme application per year Learn more and apply here Why You Should Apply These TWAS opportunities offer: Fully funded international research experience Access to world-class institutions Career advancement in science and academia Global collaboration opportunities Exposure to advanced research environments Application Deadlines Summary TWAS-DFG (SSA): April 15, 2026 TWAS-DFG (MENA): April 15, 2026 TWAS-CSIR PhD: May 6, 2026 Discover more global Fellowship and Scholarship opportunities on Opportunities for Youth (OFY). The post TWAS Fellowships Research Opportunities 2026: Fully Funded PhD, Postdoctoral Visits to Germany, and Global Research Collaboration Programs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Fully Funded UN Climate Change Youth Event 2026: Apply Now for the ACE Hub Youth Event (Global Opportunity for Young Climate Leaders)
summary: If you are a young climate advocate, environmental innovator, or aspiring global change-maker, the 2026 Action for Climate Empowerment (ACE) Hub Youth Event offers one of the most impactful international platforms to elevate your voice, build your skills, and collaborate with global peers. Organized under the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC), this prestigious event is designed to empower youth to lead meaningful climate action at all levels. What Is the ACE Hub Youth Event? The ACE Hub Youth Event is a flagship international gathering under the broader Action for Climate Empowerment (ACE) Hub, a global initiative focused on equipping individuals—especially youth—with the knowledge, skills, and tools to actively participate in climate action. The ACE Hub itself is a collaboration between UN Climate Change and the Government of North Rhine-Westphalia in Germany, supporting the implementation of climate education, public awareness, training, and participation under the Paris Agreement. This annual youth event is specifically designed to: Build the capacity of young leaders to drive climate solutions Promote collaboration and innovation in climate initiatives Facilitate global networking and knowledge exchange Strengthen youth engagement in policy and grassroots action Why You Should Apply Participating in the ACE Hub Youth Event is not just about attending a conference—it is about becoming part of a global movement shaping climate policy and action. Key Benefits Gain hands-on training in climate advocacy and project design Engage with international experts and policymakers Collaborate with youth leaders from across the world Strengthen your leadership and communication skills Expand your global network in climate and sustainability Opportunity to attend sessions linked to UN climate negotiations Participants also benefit from immersive learning experiences including workshops, peer-led sessions, and expert-led discussions. Who Can Apply? (Eligibility Criteria) This opportunity targets young climate leaders and changemakers who are actively engaged in climate-related initiatives. Eligible Category of People Youth leaders Climate activists and advocates Environmental professionals Students and early-career researchers Community organizers working on climate solutions General Requirements Applicants must: Be aged 18 to 35 years Have proficiency in English (spoken and written) Be able to attend the full event in person in Bonn, Germany Demonstrate interest or experience in climate action Be engaged in areas such as: Climate education Public awareness Community-based climate initiatives Policy advocacy Geographic Eligibility Open to all countries worldwide Additional slots available for local youth from North Rhine-Westphalia (Germany) Program Structure and Activities The ACE Hub Youth Event is structured as a 3-day intensive experience combining learning, collaboration, and innovation. What to Expect Day 1: Skills-building workshops and peer-led sessions Day 2: Collaborative group work on climate solutions Day 3: Networking, presentations, and knowledge exchange Participants will: Present their own climate initiatives Work on solving real-world environmental challenges Participate in expert-led sessions on communication and advocacy The program emphasizes interactive learning and practical application, ensuring participants leave with actionable skills. Themes and Focus Areas The 2026 edition continues to focus on youth leadership and climate entrepreneurship, with strong emphasis on: Community-based climate action Innovation and sustainable solutions Climate communication and advocacy Local-to-global climate impact strategies Funding and Financial Support While the event includes both funded and self-funded participation options: A limited number of international participants may receive funding support Additional participants may attend through self-funding or independent accreditation Funding (when available) may cover: Travel expenses Accommodation Participation costs Why This Opportunity Matters Climate change is one of the most urgent global challenges of our time, and youth are at the forefront of driving solutions. The ACE Hub Youth Event directly contributes to empowering young people under Article 6 of the UNFCCC and Article 12 of the Paris Agreement, which focus on education, awareness, and participation. By participating, you are not only gaining skills—you are becoming part of a global ecosystem working toward sustainable and equitable climate solutions. How to Apply To apply for the ACE Hub Youth Event 2026: Complete the official online application form Provide details of your climate-related experience Demonstrate your motivation and impact potential Submit before the deadline Apply here Deadline 10th April 2026 The ACE Hub Youth Event 2026 in Bonn, Germany is more than just a conference—it is a transformative experience for young individuals committed to climate action. Whether you are already leading initiatives or just starting your journey, this opportunity provides the platform, resources, and network needed to amplify your impact. For more information click here For more global opportunities click here The post Fully Funded UN Climate Change Youth Event 2026: Apply Now for the ACE Hub Youth Event (Global Opportunity for Young Climate Leaders) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Anzisha Coach Job Opportunity 2026 (Remote) at African Leadership Academy: Empower Africa’s Next Generation of Entrepreneurs
summary: If you are passionate about entrepreneurship, youth development, and creating meaningful impact across Africa, the Anzisha Coach position at African Leadership Academy offers a unique and transformative career path. This role sits at the intersection of education and entrepreneurship, two of the most critical drivers of economic growth and innovation across the African continent. Through its flagship initiative, Anzisha Program , the Academy is shaping a generation of young entrepreneurs who are building sustainable businesses and creating jobs in their communities. The position is ideal for experienced professionals who want to mentor, coach, and directly influence the growth of early-stage entrepreneurs across Africa. About the Anzisha Program The Anzisha Program is a partnership-driven initiative supported by organizations such as Mastercard Foundation . It focuses on empowering very young entrepreneurs (typically under 22 years old) to build and scale impactful businesses. The program consists of three key stages: 1. Idea Program (3 Months) A foundational stage where young entrepreneurs: Develop a Minimum Viable Product (MVP) Validate business ideas with real-world sales or impact Prepare for scaling opportunities 2. Build Program (5 Months) Focused on: Strengthening business models Driving measurable growth (revenue, jobs, impact) Building sustainable and scalable ventures 3. Venture Building Fellowship A multi-year fellowship that: Provides continuous business and leadership support Recognizes outstanding young entrepreneurs Helps scale ventures across markets Role Overview: What Does an Anzisha Coach Do? As an Anzisha Coach, you will play a pivotal role in shaping Africa’s entrepreneurial ecosystem by working directly with young founders. This is not just a coaching role—it is a high-impact leadership position involving mentorship, program design, ecosystem building, and strategic execution. You will: Provide one-on-one coaching Support entrepreneurs in business development Facilitate training sessions and workshops Connect founders to networks, partners, and investors Track progress using data-driven insights Key Responsibilities Explained in Detail 1. Coaching and Mentorship You will serve as a trusted advisor to young entrepreneurs by: Guiding business strategy, financial planning, and marketing execution Helping founders navigate real-world challenges Building strong, empathy-driven relationships Supporting participants across multiple programs, including AL for Education initiatives You will also travel across African countries to: Visit entrepreneurs Understand local business environments Provide in-person support where necessary 2. Program Development and Project Management This role requires strong operational leadership. You will: Design and implement entrepreneurship programs Lead workshops on leadership and business growth Develop innovative support strategies for young founders Manage projects end-to-end, from concept to execution Additionally, you will: Deliver tailored interventions with external partners Contribute to monthly fellowship reports Continuously improve program effectiveness 3. Networking and Ecosystem Building A major part of your work will involve building connections across Africa’s entrepreneurial ecosystem: Link entrepreneurs to industry experts, mentors, and investors Identify strategic partners across different countries Support expansion of the Anzisha network 4. Talent Identification and Selection You will help discover the next generation of entrepreneurs by: Identifying promising candidates Supporting the selection process Reviewing and grading applications and assignments 5. Tracking, Evaluation, and Reporting Data-driven decision-making is central to this role. You will: Monitor entrepreneur performance Track progress against KPIs Provide detailed performance reports Use insights to improve program delivery 6. Collaboration and Thought Leadership As a visible representative of the program, you will: Share insights through articles, podcasts, and events Participate in outreach activities and information sessions Support content creation and storytelling Conduct research on challenges facing African entrepreneurs Minimum Requirements and Qualifications Educational Background Bachelor’s degree in: Business Entrepreneurship Or a related field A Master’s degree is an added advantage Professional Experience Candidates must have: Proven experience as: Entrepreneur Business consultant Coach Strong understanding of entrepreneurship principles Experience working with young people Demonstrated leadership and emotional intelligence Core Skills Required Project management and execution Data analysis and KPI tracking Problem-solving and critical thinking Communication and interpersonal skills Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities Preferred Qualifications Financial expertise and investor network Experience running a business in Africa Multilingual ability (especially French) Strong stakeholder management skills Competencies and Personal Attributes To succeed in this role, candidates must demonstrate: Technical Competencies Coaching and mentorship Program development Networking and partnerships Monitoring and evaluation Personal Attributes High integrity and professionalism Emotional maturity and resilience Strong interpersonal and conflict-resolution skills Ability to work under pressure Initiative and proactive mindset Who Can Apply? Eligible Candidates This opportunity is open to: Professionals with mid to senior-level experience Individuals passionate about entrepreneurship and youth development Candidates with experience working in or with African markets Geographic Eligibility Open to applicants across Africa Remote role, but: Preference is given to candidates based in South Africa Must be willing to travel across African countries Apply here Why This Opportunity Matters This is more than a job—it is a chance to: Shape Africa’s entrepreneurial future Mentor the next generation of business leaders Work within a high-impact, pan-African organization Contribute to job creation and economic development By joining the African Leadership Academy , you become part of a mission-driven ecosystem committed to transforming Africa through leadership and innovation. Deadline 17th April 2026 The Anzisha Coach role is ideal for individuals who combine entrepreneurial experience, coaching ability, and a passion for impact. It offers a rare opportunity to influence both individual lives and broader economic systems across Africa.
